steps.title: Extract the radius from the diameter
steps.content:
The diameter is 6 inches. Since radius is half the diameter:
$$r = \frac{6}{2} = 3 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Calculate the volume using the cylinder formula
steps.content:
Use the cylinder volume formula with $r = 3$ and $h = 21$:
$$V = \pi(3)^2(21) = \pi(9)(21) = 189\pi \text{ cubic inches}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
